paragraphs.text: Dear the Commission, I am writing to you on my subject on the Digital Fairness Act (Stop killing games) initiative. In my view, this initiative is per consumer and supports my right products I bought with my money and be made available to me without a deadline or expiry date. Moreover, the publisher’s reserved right is my product that I bought fairly from him without reason to make it clearer that consumer protection laws have already been laid down in the EU for other goods. The consequences of the game that derive from it can no longer be maintained by the Puplisher and I am aware and should be taken into consideration because this is understandable, but this must of course be communicated in the same way as traditional software which gives support, and I can also say from my experience as a computer for application development that it is technically cheaper for the publisher to equip a game without or with limited online functionality, as the many constant services that are needed to insure a lot of online game or an online game consume a lot of electricity, hardware and time, and thus cost a lot of money. I hope that my feedback will provide a clearer picture of the initiative and I will clarify any questions that may arise and set out my position clearly. Yours sincerely, Eduard Lobes
